beats.activity: Wake up, strong coffee, check team Discord and weather radar.
beats.reasoning: Creature of habit starts with ritual coffee and team comms; drizzly rain forecasted so indoor morning makes sense. Yesterday ended low-energy (40/100), so a grounded start at home is appropriate.

beats.activity: Commute to Interbay, avoiding SR-520 eastbound closure by taking surface streets.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; leaving early (7:50am) and routing via surface streets avoids the closure and gets to work before the bottleneck. Wet roads add 5–10 min buffer.

beats.activity: Coach esports team: morning scrims, replay analysis, mid-laner overextension follow-up.
beats.reasoning: Full morning/early afternoon work block. Noted yesterday that mid-laner needs coaching; rainy weather keeps this indoors. Rain ends around 2pm, so timing works.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby café; quick bite before afternoon session.
beats.reasoning: Stays in Interbay for lunch to minimize commute; rain is still steady at noon, so indoor meal keeps routine smooth.

beats.activity: Afternoon coaching: team strategy review, mental prep for upcoming tournament.
beats.reasoning: Extended afternoon work block; rain clears by 2pm, but team is mid-focus so staying put. Competitive schedule requires intensive coaching.

beats.activity: Commute home; surface streets clear as rain ends, stopping briefly at fly shop in Ballard.
beats.reasoning: Light detour to Ballard fly shop (familiar haunt from yesterday) on way home; rain cleared, so pleasant 15-min walk through Ballard. Fits habit of picking up materials.

beats.activity: Continue home from Ballard to Fremont.
beats.reasoning: Final commute leg; clear skies now, allows time to decompress from work day.

beats.activity: Stop at Pike Place Market for fresh fish and vegetables for dinner prep.
beats.reasoning: Slight detour from Ballard to Fremont via Pike Place (familiar from yesterday); rain cleared so market is pleasant. Values fresh ingredients for home cooking.

beats.activity: Final commute home; walk with groceries.
beats.reasoning: Completes journey home with evening light and groceries in hand.

diary: Woke to rain drumming on the roof—the kind of Tuesday that makes you want to stay under the covers. But the team needed me, so I grabbed strong coffee and checked Discord while the weather radar painted everything gray. The SR-520 closure meant dodging the usual route; I took surface streets through Fremont and hit Interbay just as the drizzle picked up. Morning scrims were rough—our mid-laner kept overextending, and I spent half the session breaking down replays with him. By lunch at the café, I was feeling the weight of it. Afternoon was better; we reset, ran strategy drills, got everyone's head right for the tournament push. On the way home, the rain finally quit, so I stopped at the fly shop in Ballard—needed to clear my head, picked up some new leaders. Ended up swinging by Pike Place for fresh fish and greens. Something about handling good ingredients, prepping dinner with my hands, felt grounding after a day of screens and strategy calls.
thought_process: The rain and traffic closure set the tone early—I knew it'd be a grinding day, so I made sure to fuel up and check routes before leaving. The team's performance struggles weighed on me more than usual; I think it's the tournament pressure building. I decided to lean into the replay analysis instead of just correcting behavior, hoping they'd own the mistakes rather than just hear criticism. By afternoon, I could feel my own frustration settling, so I deliberately added the fly shop stop—not planned, but I needed it. Those few minutes looking at gear helped reset my nervous system. The Pike Place detour felt intentional too; I was hungry for something real after so much digital coaching. Ended the day feeling more like myself, less like a frustrated voice in a Discord channel.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
